title: Precomputed Constants — L1 Cache Is Sacred
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Precomputing frequently-used constants (SQRT3, INV_SQRT3) and pinning them in L1 cache eliminates redundant FP operations in hot paths
content.proof: SQRT(3.0) takes ~14 cycles on modern FPU. A precomputed REAL64 parameter is a register load: 1 cycle. At 2.27B ops/sec, saving 13 cycles per iteration compounds to massive throughput gains.
content.benchmark: With precomputed constants: 2.27B ops/sec. Without: ~1.1B ops/sec (estimated)
content.caveat: Only matters in tight loops. For control flow, compute-on-use is fine.
